content_hints.description: The passage from the landing chamber opens into a vaulted hall whose ceiling has partially collapsed, leaving rubble in piles that create natural chokepoints. Torchlight from the landing behind you fades within thirty feet, replaced by phosphorescent fungus that casts everything in sickly green. Boot prints in the dust lead in every direction -- dozens of parties have passed through here. Most of the tracks only go one way.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step into a vaulted hall where the ceiling has partially collapsed, leaving mounds of rubble that narrow the path ahead. Phosphorescent fungus clings to the walls in pale green patches, casting a sickly light that makes shadows pool in every crevice. Dust on the floor is churned with boot prints heading in every direction.
event_triggers: On entry: Scratching sounds in the walls. The rats have learned that the pulley system means fresh meat.

content_hints.description: Fungi forest paths run between patches of stout trillimac and a forest of towering zurkhwood whose caps scrape the 40-foot-high ceiling. Throughout the cavern are small clearings where the fungi have been torn out of the mossy ground.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see colored lanterns strung on fraying rope transforming this cavern into a chaotic underground bazaar. Makeshift stalls of scrap wood and salvaged doors crowd the floor, and small green figures hawk their wares in shrill, overlapping voices. The air is thick with the smell of roasting meat of uncertain origin and perfume applied in quantities that could fell an owlbear.

npcs.name: Yek the Tall
npcs.dialogue: Welcome to Yek's! Best prices on Level 1, guaranteed! You want sword? We got sword. You want information? We got information. You want directions to Level 2? That costs extra. Yek knows the way. Yek knows ALL the ways.
npcs.notes: Goblin boss. Wears a battered top hat and a monocle with no lens. Runs the Goblin Bazaar as a neutral zone -- no fighting allowed, enforced by twenty goblins with crossbows in the balcony above. Shrewd trader who accepts coin, barter, or 'interesting stories.'

event_triggers: On browse: A goblin child tugs your sleeve and whispers: 'The bugbears in the east hall are digging for something. Something old. Yek does not want you to know that.'

content_hints.description: The main cave has a 40-foot-high ceiling filled with petrified objects. A statue of a princely human clad in plate armor, with his sword drawn, lies among the ruins.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a curved set of stone stairs climbing twenty feet to the upper level, covered in bird droppings, broken roof shingles, mud, and mold. The air is thick with the smell of decay, and you notice a fireplace blackened with soot with a burned heap of padded leather chair lying near it.

loot.name: Adventurer's Pack (salvaged, contains waterskin and 3 rations)

event_triggers: On stealth success (DC 13): The bugbears can be bypassed entirely while sleeping.

content_hints.description: This area features a Green Slime covering sections of the vertical wall, obscuring a secret door that can be found by scraping the slime off the wall. Nearby, an altar of variegated gray stone is visible, and the panels weigh 50 pounds each.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see three stone tables arranged in a U-shape, their surfaces stained dark with fluids you would rather not identify. The walls are honeycombed with niches, each holding a wrapped bundle roughly the size and shape of a body—some yellowed with age, others disturbingly fresh. Candles drip wax onto every surface, and a robed figure hunches over the nearest table, working with small, precise tools.

npcs.name: Midna Tauberth
npcs.dialogue: The dead have no need of their possessions. I redistribute them to the living. Call it recycling. The teeth? Components. You do not want to know what for.
npcs.notes: Human undertaker, neutral evil. Has set up shop in the dungeon to harvest corpses left by adventurers. Sells salvaged gear at fair prices. Will buy bodies (5 gp each, no questions asked). Knows the layout of Level 1 from following the dead.

content_hints.description: Hobgoblins of Doomcrown's phalanx have secured most of the halls and chambers in Arcturiadoom, leaving only wandering monsters of consequence. The level 1 features include spells such as Fireball, Hypnotic Pattern, and Detect Magic, alongside various combat and utility options.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a cluttered workshop stretching into shadow, where workbenches sag under half-finished contraptions. Arcane formulae cover the walls, the ceiling, and the floor, scrawled in handwriting that starts precise and degenerates into frantic, looping scribbles at the edges. The air hums with residual magic, and small objects on the benches twitch at your approach.

loot.name: Mirror of the Past (shows yesterday's events in reflected room)

hazards.name: Wild magic zone (roll on Wild Magic Surge table on spell cast)

event_triggers: On notes read: Halaster's handwriting includes margin notes addressed to future readers: 'If you are reading this, you are already on Level 1, which means you are already doomed. Welcome!'

content_hints.description: The area features a temple of Dumathoin, converted into a vile hatchery for giant spiders by T'rissa Auvryn­dar and her followers. A drow priestess stands atop a raised dais presiding over a group of four drow and three giant spiders.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see the corridor ahead barricaded with salvaged dungeon furniture; doors are propped as walls, tables turned on their sides as parapets, and chair legs sharpened into stakes jutting outward. Crossbow bolts glint in the torchlight behind the barricade, aimed at you. A crudely painted sign hangs above the fortification: TOLL: 10 GP PER HEAD. HEADS ACCEPTED AS ALTERNATE PAYMENT.

npcs.name: Grum'shar
npcs.dialogue: Ten gold or ten toes. Your choice. The Guild controls this corridor and everything south of it. You want to go deeper, you pay. You want to argue, Grum'shar can argue too.
npcs.notes: Half-orc warlock. Xanathar Guild lieutenant. Secretly has an intellect devourer planted in a nearby prisoner, ready to deploy if things go wrong. Cruel but pragmatic -- will negotiate if outmatched.

loot.name: Guild Toll Chest (65 gp in mixed coin)

event_triggers: On combat: Grum'shar activates the intellect devourer as a bonus action on round 2.

content_hints.description: A natural cavern where rats have achieved civilization, or at least its crude beginnings. Crude tunnels honeycomb the walls, each barely wide enough for a child.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a tankard in the other. A shattered wooden cask lies nearby. Careful examination of the wall above the skeleton reveals a slot carved into the stone.

loot.name: Ring of Swimming (in rat nest refuse)

event_triggers: On Speak with Animals: The rats are terrified. 'The metal ones from below. They come at night. They take us. They put things in our heads.'

content_hints.description: A 20-foot-high, 20-foot-wide stone corridor ends before an adamantine double door bearing Dwarvish runes that read, “THETHREE KEYS. BRING THEM FORTH.” This area contains a 10-foot-diameter circle of runes drawn on the floor in dried blood, smoke billowing from four iron braziers, and a barlgura slumped motionless within the circle.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see a 20-foot-high, 20-foot-wide stone corridor ending before an adamantine double door bearing Dwarvish runes. The writing on them reads, “THETHREE KEYS. BRING THEM FORTH.”

loot.name: Stolen Goods (120 gp in assorted valuables)

event_triggers: On combat start: The chieftain kicks the throne backward and draws his morningstar. 'Yek said you were coming. Yek is a snitch. But you -- you are dinner.'
On defeat: The arch flares to life, its runes blazing blue-white. The stairway down is open. Somewhere far below, something laughs.
